name: Thayer and Spring
address: 915 Silver Spring Ave, Silver Spring, MD 20910
bathroomsNote: 2BR units have 2 baths; some units feature garden tubs or separate glass-wall showers; select units have twin-basin vanities
approxRentRange: $2,510 to $3,516/month (2BR market rate; current promotion up to one month free on 12-month lease signed by 7/15/26)

notes.doorman: Brookfield's official listing calls it '24/7 Concierge' and resident reviews call staff professional; however multiple reviews note high concierge turnover and occasional unstaffed desk. Elevated to 'Likely staffed' rather than 'Confirmed 24h' because staffing gaps are documented.
notes.elevator: Building is 11 stories; elevators confirmed present but multiple reviews cite frequent breakdowns.
notes.balcony: Select units only. Floor plan names include '2E w/ Balcony,' '2F w/ Balcony,' '2M w/ Balcony'; also 'private patios or entrances' in some ground-level units. Not standard across all apartments.
notes.inUnitLaundry: In-home washer/dryer listed as a unit feature; RentCafe notes 'select apartments' for premium laundry configurations, but washer/dryer appears broadly standard.
notes.petFriendly: Cats and dogs welcome; on-site pet spa; breed restrictions apply per typical Brookfield policy.
notes.packageRoom: Secured package lockers plus concierge package acceptance.
notes.unitClimate: Air conditioning listed as a unit feature across multiple listing aggregators, but no source specifies whether thermostats are resident-controlled or building-controlled. Marked Unclear.
notes.has2BRor1BRDen: 2BR floor plans confirmed. No 1BR+den floor plan found in any source.
notes.wellMaintained: Common areas described as clean; Brookfield is a large institutional operator (built 2019, 399 units). RentCafe aggregate rating 3.5/5 across 277 reviews. Recurring elevator reliability complaints pull against a 'Yes' rating.
notes.buildingDetails: Built 2019; 11 stories; 399 units; studios, 1BR, 2BR, and townhomes; managed by Brookfield Properties. MPDU and workforce housing units available alongside market-rate units.
